channel.title: Health Care
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: RFK Jr.'s olive branch isn't winning over his staffers
item.description: But inside the health department, workers say the dysfunction of the DOGE era persists.

media:content.media:title: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his deputies appear to be taking a gentler approach with their health department staff. But softer rhetoric doesn't necessarily reflect deeper changes to department operations.

item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner

item.title: Trump wants you to know this RFK Jr. aide’s name
item.description: Chris Klomp is working to boost morale and deliver results at the Health Department after a year of upheaval.

media:content.media:title: Klomp has overseen personnel moves and has been driving a focus on healthy eating, improving health care affordability and fighting fraud at HHS. Kent Nishimura

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun

item.title: America’s doctors just voted for war with RFK Jr.
item.description: The American Medical Association has sought a working relationship with the health secretary. Members saw moral compromise.

media:content.media:title: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is facing renewed criticism from the country's largest association of physicians.

item.dc:creator: By Simon J. Levien

item.title: Bill Gates’ day of reckoning on Epstein has arrived
item.description: Lawmakers will question the billionaire Microsoft founder and global health philanthropist about his ties to the late sex offender.

media:content.media:title: Microsoft's Bill Gates attends a dinner with President Donald Trump in the State Dining Room of the White House in Washington, on Sept. 4, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun and Hailey Fuchs
item.dc:contributor: By Carmen Paun and Hailey Fuchs

item.title: How sick is sick enough? New Medicaid work rule worries patient advocates, states.
item.description: Trump administration wants ill recipients to prove they can’t work every six months. Doctors, advocates and state officials wonder how.

media:content.media:title: CMS Administrator Mehmet Oz cheered a new rule for work requirements as creating a path to prosperity for Americans, but doctors and patient groups worry the sickest patients may lose coverage.

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and Robert King
item.dc:contributor: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and Robert King

item.title: How do you stop Ebola at the World Cup?
item.description: Public-health officials are trying to use a Covid-era playbook without pandemic-era funding.

media:content.media:title: Kaitlyn Bertolino, left, sets up a monitor while fellow nurse Nicholas Pena administers oxygen to a manikin during a NYC Health + Hospitals exercise at Bellevue Hospital in New York, on June 2, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Rachel Bluth, Sophie Gardner, Gelila Negesse, Arek Sarkissian and Maya Kaufman
item.dc:contributor: By Rachel Bluth, Sophie Gardner, Gelila Negesse, Arek Sarkissian and Maya Kaufman

item.title: RFK Jr.’s movement was supposed to save the GOP’s majorities. It’s not even in the game.
item.description: Make America Healthy Again groups have endorsed only one candidate in a competitive congressional race.

media:content.media:title: White House aides told POLITICO last year they hoped Kennedy's supporters would help the GOP retain control of the House and Senate.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu

item.title: 2 researchers charged with smuggling mpox into the US
item.description: Employed at a National Institutes of Health lab in Montana, the two allegedly brought deactivated virus from the Republic of the Congo without a permit.

media:content.media:title: A 2022 outbreak of mpox, formerly known as monkeypox, reached the United States, infecting more than 30,000 and killing 42, according to the CDC.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun

item.title: Milk’s the drink of choice at RFK Jr.’s event with swing-district Republican
item.description: The health secretary appeared at a Wisconsin dairy farm with embattled Rep. Derrick Van Orden.

media:content.media:title: Kennedy is just the latest Cabinet secretary to appear with swing-district Rep. Derrick Van Orden (in red hat).

item.dc:creator: By Simon J. Levien

item.title: States balk at the high price of Medicaid work requirements amid budget crunch
item.description: States dealing with budget shortfalls are facing tens of millions of dollars in new costs ahead of the federal Jan. 1 deadline.

media:content.media:title: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act signed into law in July 2025 called on all Medicaid expansion states to implement work requirements by Jan. 1, 2027. States say they will have to find millions to update their systems to implement the law.

item.dc:creator: By Robert King and Alice Miranda Ollstein
item.dc:contributor: By Robert King and Alice Miranda Ollstein

item.title: Ebola response puts Trump on collision course with global health body
item.description: The Trump administration has implemented some of the United States’ most stringent travel restrictions for infectious disease ever.

media:content.media:title: Though most countries are following the World Health Organization's advice on travel bans, the U.S. is far from alone in ignoring it.

item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner and Carmen Paun
item.dc:contributor: By Sophie Gardner and Carmen Paun

item.title: Montana senator wants monkey bite, and lab where it happened, investigated
item.description: Tim Sheehy’s request comes after a monkey with Crimean-Congo hemorrhagic fever bit a researcher at the Rocky Mountain Laboratories.

media:content.media:title: Sheehy wants reassurance a lab in his state won't be the source of a leak.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun and Megan Messerly
item.dc:contributor: By Carmen Paun and Megan Messerly

item.title: RFK Jr., states at odds over cause of Obamacare’s enrollment declines
item.description: Trump administration officials point to their work on fraud as the reason for dropoffs while states and insurers blame higher premiums.

media:content.media:title: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. recently told Congress that the people who lost ACA coverage were not entitled to it in the first place.

item.dc:creator: By Robert King and Kelly Hooper
item.dc:contributor: By Robert King and Kelly Hooper

item.title: Anti-abortion activists' frustrations escalate after abortion pill ruling
item.description: Abortion opponents are demanding action from the FDA and other federal agencies.

media:content.media:credit: Bryan Dozier/Middle East Images/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: Anti-abortion demonstrators march to the Supreme Court, which recently declined to cut off access to abortion pills via telemedicine.

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein

item.title: US well-positioned to deal with current Ebola outbreak, Deborah Birx says
item.description: She said the country has "a deep bench" even in federal agencies without a confirmed leader.

media:content.media:title: Red Cross workers bury an Ebola victim at the Rwampara Cemetery in Rwampara, Congo, on May 23, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By David Cohen

item.title: CDC says green card holders who were recently in countries where Ebola is spreading can’t reenter US
item.description: The move expands existing travel restrictions barring foreigners who’ve recently been in Congo, South Sudan and Uganda.

media:content.media:title: A health worker disinfects an Ebola treatment center in Rwampara, Congo, May 21, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner

item.title: Relief group is phasing out vaccines RFK Jr. believes are unsafe
item.description: The health secretary has blocked $600 million for Gavi, which provides shots to poor countries, because of his concerns about a mercury-containing preservative.

media:content.media:title: Gavi has provided HHS a plan to accelerate a shift to new vaccines. Fabrice COFFRINI

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun

item.title: RFK Jr. announces 'the largest autism fraud bust in American history' and it’s in Minnesota
item.description: The arrests have the Trump administration again accusing Gov. Tim Walz of poor oversight of federal funds.

media:content.media:title: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. speaks alongside CMS Administrator Mehmet Oz and and Assistant Attorney General Colin McDonald during a press conference Thursday in Minneapolis.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun and Robert King
item.dc:contributor: By Carmen Paun and Robert King

item.title: RFK Jr. fires leaders of group that determines what insurers must cover
item.description: The health secretary has said the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force erred by failing to recommend screening for Alzheimer's disease.

media:content.media:title: The firing of task force members has raised concerns that Kennedy wants the panel to endorse his personal views about what’s preventive.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun

item.title: Pharma’s legal assault on Medicare negotiations hits a SCOTUS wall
item.description: Supreme Court decision not to hear pharma cases gives “strong signal” that Medicare price talks will continue.

media:content.media:title: The U.S. Supreme Court building is seen in Washington, on May 8, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Lauren Gardner and David Lim
item.dc:contributor: By Lauren Gardner and David Lim

item.title: Relief group says Trump cuts ‘led to delayed detection of deadly Ebola outbreak’
item.description: The International Rescue Committee said the cuts are partially to blame for the rapid spread of the disease in the Congo.

media:content.media:title: Ebola cases have already emerged in major regional cities in Congo and Uganda.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun

item.title: Abortion clinic protesters eligible for payouts from new Trump ‘anti-weaponization’ fund
item.description: The new fund comes as a pro-abortion rights group says threats and violent attacks at clinics are on the rise.

media:content.media:title: The Trump administration last year pardoned dozens of anti-abortion activists who had violated the 1994 FACE Act. They may now receive financial compensation. (Photo by Kent Nishimura/Getty Images)

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein

item.title: Congo opens more centers to treat rare type of Ebola that has killed more than 110
item.description: “We are playing catch-up against a very dangerous pathogen," one expert said.

media:content.media:title: Medical supplies are stacked inside a World Health Organization (WHO) warehouse in Nairobi, Kenya, on May 18, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Associated Press

item.title: CDC announces 30-day travel restrictions in response to Ebola outbreak
item.description: The agency is restricting entry into the U.S. for non-U.S. passport holders who have visited areas where they may have been exposed to the virus.

media:content.media:title: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is temporarily suspending entry into the U.S. for travelers who have visited Ebola outbreak areas. That restriction does not apply to U.S. citizens, U.S. nationals or lawful permanent residents.

item.dc:creator: By Sophie Gardner

item.title: Ebola risk in US remains low amid Congo outbreak, CDC says
item.description: The World Health Organization has declared the outbreak in central Africa “a public health emergency of international concern.”

media:content.media:title: A health official uses a thermometer to screen people in front of Kibuli Muslim Hospital in Kampala, Uganda, on May 16, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Kelly Hooper

item.title: 1 of RFK Jr.’s biggest fans in the Senate wants Bill Cassidy’s job
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall, a Make America Healthy Again devotee, is angling to replace the defeated Louisiana senator atop the Health Committee.

media:content.media:title: Marshall, a doctor before he got into politics, has told Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. he shares his skepticism of the U.S. vaccine schedule.

item.dc:creator: By Simon J. Levien

item.title: Hospitals are taking the fall for high health care costs
item.description: On right and left and among industry rivals, everyone is pointing the finger at the caregivers.

media:content.media:title: A barrage of attacks against hospitals marks the latest high-profile clash among three of Washington’s most powerful lobbies.

item.dc:creator: By Amanda Chu

item.title: RFK Jr.’s department to make it easier to fire career staff
item.description: Managers received notices Friday that job classifications were changing.

media:content.media:title: Trump critics believe the job reclassifications are meant to make it easier to purge staffers who aren’t loyal to the president.

item.dc:creator: By Carmen Paun and Sophie Gardner
item.dc:contributor: By Carmen Paun and Sophie Gardner

item.title: Acting FDA leader tries to explain past Planned Parenthood work to abortion opponents
item.description: Kyle Diamantas, who was elevated to FDA acting commissioner earlier this week, is telling anti-abortion groups he asked to be taken off a case defending Planned Parenthood due to moral objections.

media:content.media:title: FDA Deputy Commissioner for Human Foods Kyle Diamantas prepares for an interview with POLITICO and Axel Springer in a conference room in the Harvey W. Wiley Federal Building in College Park, Maryland, on Dec. 16, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and David Lim
item.dc:contributor: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and David Lim

item.title: Supreme Court extends order maintaining abortion pill access
item.description: Justices Clarence Thomas and Samuel Alito dissented from the majority decision.

media:content.media:title: The fate of abortion access is one again back before the U.S. Supreme Court. (Photo by DREW ANGERER/AFP via Getty Images)

item.dc:creator: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and Josh Gerstein
item.dc:contributor: By Alice Miranda Ollstein and Josh Gerstein
